symptom: a firm swelling low at the base of the spine that the pregnancy scan already flagged
hook: a large mass at the base of the spine was flecked with tiny calcium spots and small pockets of fat
whatYouSee: A large mass at the base of the spine flecked with scattered calcifications and small pockets of fat.
whyItMatters: A tumor made of several tissue types has been growing near the tailbone since early in the pregnancy and can even contain fragments of tooth or bone.
treatment: Surgeons remove the whole mass along with the tailbone and then follow the child for any regrowth.
takeaway: A tumor can carry pieces of tissue that have no business being there.

generated.threadsCaption:
A patient came in with a firm swelling low at the base of the spine that the pregnancy scan already flagged.

Then the X-ray loaded 😭

And a large mass at the base of the spine was flecked with tiny calcium spots and small pockets of fat.

Case #88 🩻 Difficulty 4/5

Medics: your diagnosis?

Everyone else: What surprised you most about where this mass sits?

Answer in 45 min 👀 no spoilers from me till then
generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Sacrococcygeal teratoma

👀 What you see:
A large mass at the base of the spine flecked with scattered calcifications and small pockets of fat.

🦴 Why it matters:
A tumor made of several tissue types has been growing near the tailbone since early in the pregnancy and can even contain fragments of tooth or bone.

💊 Treatment:

Surgeons remove the whole mass along with the tailbone and then follow the child for any regrowth.
generated.ctaText:

laypersonQuestion: What surprised you most about where this mass sits?
seedHint: Look at the base of the spine and count the bright flecks. Solid or hollow?
